displayTitle: Flaw or Defect: The Last Link in the Chain

beats.narration: Your system can balance, filter, and display perfectly, and the test still fails if the disposition is wrong. The output is a decision.

payload.title: FLAW OR DEFECT?
payload.sub: The last link is the call you make from the signal.

beats.narration: These words carry contractual weight. An indication you interpret. A discontinuity you find. A flaw is detectable but not necessarily rejectable. Only one term means reject.
Only one thing turns a flaw into a defect: written acceptance criteria. They come from the code, never from you, translated into instrument terms.
Classify before you dispose. Repeat it, place it, read phase, read amplitude, match the signature. Then go-no-go gates one threshold; sorting holds two-sided windows.
A flaw is detectable; a defect fails the criteria. Record the numbers that prove it. A reject you cannot reconstruct cannot be defended.

payload.sub: Phase, volts, percent through-wall, location — every call traces to the criteria.
